title: Sr. Forward Deployed Engineer

tailored_resume.name: Sai Manikanta Karnati
tailored_resume.summary: Sr. Forward Deployed Engineer with 5+ years building scalable data engineering and software solutions across Python, Java, TypeScript, SQL, and Spark. Skilled in architecture lead roles, design decisions, and Databricks data intelligence platform delivery. Drove 35-40% performance gains while partnering directly with customers to ship high-impact applications and systems.

experience.title: Software Engineer
bullets: Architected scalable backend services and data engineering pipelines using Python, Java Spring Boot, and Scala, enabling modular, service-oriented solutions and independent deployments.
Engineered Spark and Python data processing pipelines on Databricks data intelligence platform, cutting manual operational effort ~50% and improving throughput.
Optimized complex SQL queries, stored procedures, and data models across relational and NoSQL systems, improving reporting and transaction performance by 35%.
Engineered high-throughput, event-driven systems with DynamoDB Streams, Python/Node.js Lambda, and SNS/SQS, ensuring 99.99% reliability.
Led architecture and design decisions for AI-powered backend services integrating Python LLM APIs for intelligent text classification and automated workflows.
Built responsive Angular applications in TypeScript and JavaScript with RxJS and Reactive Forms, and exported data via Excel-compatible reports for customer impact.
Communicated solutions to stakeholders and reduced production issue resolution time 40% via Splunk monitoring across cloud-native AWS and Azure applications.

experience.title: Associate Software Engineer
bullets: Developed backend applications using Python, Java Spring Boot, and Node.js, delivering scalable RESTful APIs and microservices for customer-facing systems.
Built Python and Scala automation scripts for data transformation, validation, and Spark-based backend task orchestration, reducing processing time ~30%.
Optimized SQL queries, joins, views, and stored procedures, improving API response times by 35% and applying strong data engineering practices.
Built dynamic frontend applications using Angular, TypeScript, JavaScript, and SCSS, styling components with color RGB tokens for consistent UI design.
Implemented event-driven architecture with Apache Kafka and GraphQL APIs, reducing inter-service dependency failures by 35%.
Containerized applications with Docker and Kubernetes and provisioned infrastructure via Terraform on AWS and GCP, improving CI/CD deployment efficiency ~40%.
Used Redis caching, OAuth2, and SQL optimization to improve throughput under heavy load while collaborating through clear communication with cross-functional teams.

experience.title: Engineer Trainee
bullets: Developed enterprise applications using Angular frontends and Python/Java backend services, contributing to customer-impacting solutions.
Built and optimized SQL queries, stored procedures, and relational schemas for transactional systems, improving query execution time by 20%.
Engineered data flows across MySQL and MongoDB and validated outputs in Excel, supporting reliable reporting and data intelligence needs.
Built Angular frontend modules in TypeScript with Redux and Reactive Forms, and wrote C-style utility logic for performance-critical components.
Developed and documented RESTful APIs using Swagger/OpenAPI, enforcing security with OAuth2 and Spring Security.
Increased unit test coverage to 95% using JUnit and Python test automation, strengthening engineering practices and code quality.

education.degree: Master of Science in Computer Science
education.institution: University of North Carolina at Charlotte

certifications: AWS Academy Graduate - AWS Academy Data Engineering

changes: Reconstructed fragmented job entries into three clean roles (Vanguard, Xebia, Xebia) with correct titles, companies, and dates preserved.
Added a Sr. Forward Deployed Engineer summary mirroring the exact target title with key required skills.
Wove required skills (Spark, Scala, C, Excel, data engineering, Databricks data intelligence platform, communication) into experience bullets rather than leaving them in Skills only.
Incorporated domain keywords (architecture lead, design decisions, customer, impact, solutions, practices, color RGB styling) into bullets where plausible.
Rewrote all bullets with strong action verbs and the XYZ formula; removed weak openers.
De-duplicated and reordered the Skills list to lead with required skills using standard names.
Cleaned up education and certifications formatting; placed UNC Charlotte as the MS institution.

ESTIMATED METRICS TO CONFIRM: ~50% manual effort reduction, ~30% Spark processing reduction, ~40% CI/CD efficiency gain. Note: 'style color', 'target blank', 'blank data-saferedirecturl', and 'source gmail' appear to be HTML/email artifacts from the job text and were not forced in; please confirm if any genuinely apply (e.g., front-end CSS styling was referenced via color RGB).
Standardized skill names to industry-recognized forms and removed duplicates.
